channel.title: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
channel.itunes:title: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
channel.description: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
channel.itunes:summary: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
channel.itunes:author: ccz245

item.title: Ep 1 · Ch 1 — Expanding the Self: EFIT's Attachment Blueprint
item.description:
Chapter 1: What Is EFIT? Let's See!
How EFIT reframes trauma as constriction of the self and emotional isolation rather than a symptom set, using the Henny case to introduce the three stages of Stabilization, Restructuring, and Consolidation. Listeners learn why the healing must be relational and what "expanding the self" actually means in practice.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 1: What Is EFIT? Let's See!
How EFIT reframes trauma as constriction of the self and emotional isolation rather than a symptom set, using the Henny case to introduce the three stages of Stabilization, Restructuring, and Consolidation. Listeners learn why the healing must be relational and what "expanding the self" actually means in practice.

item.title: Ep 2 · Ch 1 — Relentless Empathy: The Foundational Therapist Moves in EFIT
item.description:
Chapter 1: What Is EFIT? Let's See!
A four-step playbook for the EFIT therapist's core stance: embody A.R.E. attunement, use your own calm to slow the client's pace, anchor an embodied resilience resource, and shift from content to process with Tango Move 1. Includes red-flag markers for the rapid-fire storyteller, the dissociative shift, and the deflective maneuver.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 1: What Is EFIT? Let's See!
A four-step playbook for the EFIT therapist's core stance: embody A.R.E. attunement, use your own calm to slow the client's pace, anchor an embodied resilience resource, and shift from content to process with Tango Move 1. Includes red-flag markers for the rapid-fire storyteller, the dissociative shift, and the deflective maneuver.

item.title: Ep 3 · Ch 1 — Henny's Case: Holding the Little Girl Within
item.description:
Chapter 1: What Is EFIT? Let's See!
A full walkthrough of the Henny case, from the whirlwind of intellectualized storytelling and the church drawing with no people, to the balance-beam resilience resource, to the Stage 2 self-to-self enactment where adult Henny picks up and holds her abused child self. Shows how RISSSC and the Tango moves consolidate a corrective emotional experience.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 1: What Is EFIT? Let's See!
A full walkthrough of the Henny case, from the whirlwind of intellectualized storytelling and the church drawing with no people, to the balance-beam resilience resource, to the Stage 2 self-to-self enactment where adult Henny picks up and holds her abused child self. Shows how RISSSC and the Tango moves consolidate a corrective emotional experience.

item.title: Ep 4 · Ch 1 — Fight, Fix, Numb: Working With Protective Defenses
item.description:
Chapter 1: What Is EFIT? Let's See!
How to treat intellectualizing, topic-shifting, and humor as life-saving survival strategies to be validated rather than resistance to be broken through. Concrete moves for pacing, respecting the client's "no," and making the relationship safer than the defense.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 1: What Is EFIT? Let's See!
How to treat intellectualizing, topic-shifting, and humor as life-saving survival strategies to be validated rather than resistance to be broken through. Concrete moves for pacing, respecting the client's "no," and making the relationship safer than the defense.

item.title: Ep 5 · Ch 1 — The Therapist's Stance: Presence as the Intervention
item.description:
Chapter 1: What Is EFIT? Let's See!
Why the therapist's regulated, accessible presence is itself the trauma intervention, illustrated by Sue Johnson's softly-paced "it hurts me just to listen to you" as Henny discloses her rape. Covers the countertransference traps of clinical distance, premature fixing, and intellectualizing the horror.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 1: What Is EFIT? Let's See!
Why the therapist's regulated, accessible presence is itself the trauma intervention, illustrated by Sue Johnson's softly-paced "it hurts me just to listen to you" as Henny discloses her rape. Covers the countertransference traps of clinical distance, premature fixing, and intellectualizing the horror.

item.title: Ep 6 · Ch 2 — Five Orienting Frames and the Shift Beneath Content
item.description:
Chapter 2: What Is the Guiding Framework for EFIT?
Learn why EFIT treats the level of engagement — not the content of the problem — as the target, and how the therapist moves either meta (seeing the self-perpetuating trap) or deeper (into the felt, somatic sense). Covers the five orienting frames: experience is constructed, attachment is primary, emotion is adaptive, humans are growth-oriented, and corrective emotional experience is the route to change.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 2: What Is the Guiding Framework for EFIT?
Learn why EFIT treats the level of engagement — not the content of the problem — as the target, and how the therapist moves either meta (seeing the self-perpetuating trap) or deeper (into the felt, somatic sense). Covers the five orienting frames: experience is constructed, attachment is primary, emotion is adaptive, humans are growth-oriented, and corrective emotional experience is the route to change.

item.title: Ep 7 · Ch 2 — Making Attachment the Organizing Principle of Every Intervention
item.description:
Chapter 2: What Is the Guiding Framework for EFIT?
A four-step flow for operationalizing attachment in real time: build an A.R.E. alliance as a functional bond, listen through the attachment map for longings and fears, reframe protective strategies as reasonable rather than pathological, and co-regulate before crafting corrective moments. Includes red flags — working harder than the client, abstract talk, your own irritation — and the move for each.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 2: What Is the Guiding Framework for EFIT?
A four-step flow for operationalizing attachment in real time: build an A.R.E. alliance as a functional bond, listen through the attachment map for longings and fears, reframe protective strategies as reasonable rather than pathological, and co-regulate before crafting corrective moments. Includes red flags — working harder than the client, abstract talk, your own irritation — and the move for each.

item.title: Ep 8 · Ch 2 — Amy's Trap: When Insight Alone Isn't Enough
item.description:
Chapter 2: What Is the Guiding Framework for EFIT?
Traces Amy's two shifts — from the bird's-eye insight that guarding creates the loneliness she fears, down to the primary pain of wanting her father's approval and needing to matter — and maps the therapist's Tango moves across them. Names the trap of celebrating a client's meta-level insight when it functions as an elegant cognitive bypass.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 2: What Is the Guiding Framework for EFIT?
Traces Amy's two shifts — from the bird's-eye insight that guarding creates the loneliness she fears, down to the primary pain of wanting her father's approval and needing to matter — and maps the therapist's Tango moves across them. Names the trap of celebrating a client's meta-level insight when it functions as an elegant cognitive bypass.

item.title: Ep 9 · Ch 2 — Spotting the Yacking Loop and Auditing Your Fit
item.description:
Chapter 2: What Is the Guiding Framework for EFIT?
Four markers that a session has gone sterile — the factual report, impersonal language, the rehearsed narrative, and the venting loop — and the level-shifting moves that break each one. Also asks the honest question of therapist fit: do you actually believe emotion is adaptive information, or are you quietly treating it as a quagmire to manage?
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 2: What Is the Guiding Framework for EFIT?
Four markers that a session has gone sterile — the factual report, impersonal language, the rehearsed narrative, and the venting loop — and the level-shifting moves that break each one. Also asks the honest question of therapist fit: do you actually believe emotion is adaptive information, or are you quietly treating it as a quagmire to manage?

item.title: Ep 10 · Ch 2 — The Therapist's Internal Secure Base and the Pathology Trap
item.description:
Chapter 2: What Is the Guiding Framework for EFIT?
Applies the Tango to the therapist's own process: notice your frustration as a secondary emotion, find the fear of failure underneath, self-validate, and re-enter the room from curiosity rather than agenda. Names the Fix-it and Pathology traps, where a clinician's helplessness gets relabeled as the client's 'resistance.'
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 2: What Is the Guiding Framework for EFIT?
Applies the Tango to the therapist's own process: notice your frustration as a secondary emotion, find the fear of failure underneath, self-validate, and re-enter the room from curiosity rather than agenda. Names the Fix-it and Pathology traps, where a clinician's helplessness gets relabeled as the client's 'resistance.'

item.title: Ep 11 · Ch 3 — Becoming the Secure Base: Accessible, Responsive, Engaged
item.description:
Chapter 3: Shaping Safety: How Does the EFIT Therapist Engage the Client?
How the EFIT therapist works as a temporary attachment figure and process consultant rather than a coach or insight-giver, using Bowlby's ARE cornerstones layered onto Rogers' empathy, positive regard, and congruence. Listeners learn why intellectual reassurance and "cheerleading" send therapy down a rabbit hole, and how to step into the client's despair instead.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 3: Shaping Safety: How Does the EFIT Therapist Engage the Client?
How the EFIT therapist works as a temporary attachment figure and process consultant rather than a coach or insight-giver, using Bowlby's ARE cornerstones layered onto Rogers' empathy, positive regard, and congruence. Listeners learn why intellectual reassurance and "cheerleading" send therapy down a rabbit hole, and how to step into the client's despair instead.

item.title: Ep 12 · Ch 3 — Slowing the Polished Story Into Felt Safety
item.description:
Chapter 3: Shaping Safety: How Does the EFIT Therapist Engage the Client?
A three-phase method for building a safe haven alliance: slow-paced attunement and non-verbal tracking, direct inquiry into the client's experience of the therapeutic relationship, and choreographing a small new experience early to instil hope. Includes the red flags of a rehearsed, detached narrative and scripts for interrupting it.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 3: Shaping Safety: How Does the EFIT Therapist Engage the Client?
A three-phase method for building a safe haven alliance: slow-paced attunement and non-verbal tracking, direct inquiry into the client's experience of the therapeutic relationship, and choreographing a small new experience early to instil hope. Includes the red flags of a rehearsed, detached narrative and scripts for interrupting it.

item.title: Ep 13 · Ch 3 — Sandy's First Session: Risk Assessment as Experience
item.description:
Chapter 3: Shaping Safety: How Does the EFIT Therapist Engage the Client?
A line-by-line reading of Leanne Campbell's first session with Sandy, a client with complex relational trauma, pervasive suicidal ideation, and a fiercely intellectualized defense. Listeners see how the therapist bypasses the logic debate and choreographs an encounter with Sandy's bullied ten-year-old self to create a felt shift in the room.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 3: Shaping Safety: How Does the EFIT Therapist Engage the Client?
A line-by-line reading of Leanne Campbell's first session with Sandy, a client with complex relational trauma, pervasive suicidal ideation, and a fiercely intellectualized defense. Listeners see how the therapist bypasses the logic debate and choreographs an encounter with Sandy's bullied ten-year-old self to create a felt shift in the room.

item.title: Ep 14 · Ch 3 — Resistance as Shield: Repairing Alliance Ruptures
item.description:
Chapter 3: Shaping Safety: How Does the EFIT Therapist Engage the Client?
A protocol for meeting skepticism, "what's the use?" moments, and alliance ruptures by treating resistance as self-protection rather than obstruction. Covers de-escalating your own countertransference jab, validating the block instead of defending the model, and re-grounding the client in the roadmap.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 3: Shaping Safety: How Does the EFIT Therapist Engage the Client?
A protocol for meeting skepticism, "what's the use?" moments, and alliance ruptures by treating resistance as self-protection rather than obstruction. Covers de-escalating your own countertransference jab, validating the block instead of defending the model, and re-grounding the client in the roadmap.

item.title: Ep 15 · Ch 3 — Choreographer and Dancer: Leading While Following
item.description:
Chapter 3: Shaping Safety: How Does the EFIT Therapist Engage the Client?
How the therapist balances leading the client into deeper emotion with following their affective pace, and how to read your own somatic reactions to a client as clinical data rather than a personal failing. Includes co-regulating through breath and voice, and the traps of clinical neutrality on one side and being swept into the client's despair on the other.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 3: Shaping Safety: How Does the EFIT Therapist Engage the Client?
How the therapist balances leading the client into deeper emotion with following their affective pace, and how to read your own somatic reactions to a client as clinical data rather than a personal failing. Includes co-regulating through breath and voice, and the traps of clinical neutrality on one side and being swept into the client's despair on the other.

item.title: Ep 16 · Ch 4 — Emotion as Both Target and Agent of Change
item.description:
Chapter 4: How Does Emotion Move the Client into Change in EFIT?
Why EFIT treats emotion as the primary organizer of experience rather than a symptom, and how to read the Experiencing Scale to tell a client who is talking about pain from one who is feeling it. Includes the five EFT Tango moves that walk a client from intellectualized Level 2 narrative down into somatic, present-moment emotion.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 4: How Does Emotion Move the Client into Change in EFIT?
Why EFIT treats emotion as the primary organizer of experience rather than a symptom, and how to read the Experiencing Scale to tell a client who is talking about pain from one who is feeling it. Includes the five EFT Tango moves that walk a client from intellectualized Level 2 narrative down into somatic, present-moment emotion.

item.title: Ep 17 · Ch 4 — Assembling Emotion: Five Elements, Five Clinical Principles
item.description:
Chapter 4: How Does Emotion Move the Client into Change in EFIT?
How to collaboratively assemble a client's emotional response from its five parts (trigger, fast perception, body response, meaning, action tendency) and the five principles that govern the work: titrating risk, collaborative construction, slowing and repeating, granularity, and staying in the present. Covers somatic markers to watch for and how to respond to flooding versus numbing.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 4: How Does Emotion Move the Client into Change in EFIT?
How to collaboratively assemble a client's emotional response from its five parts (trigger, fast perception, body response, meaning, action tendency) and the five principles that govern the work: titrating risk, collaborative construction, slowing and repeating, granularity, and staying in the present. Covers somatic markers to watch for and how to respond to flooding versus numbing.

item.title: Ep 18 · Ch 4 — Kat's Starvation Diet: Restructuring an Attachment Trauma
item.description:
Chapter 4: How Does Emotion Move the Client into Change in EFIT?
A move-by-move analysis of Sessions 5 and 6 with Kat, whose anorexia and anxiety trace back to creeping into her mother's bed and finding a cold, turned back. Shows how the therapist tracks her panic somatically, uses proxy voice and co-regulating touch, and choreographs an enactment that shifts self-blame from "kid deficit" to "Mommy deficit."
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 4: How Does Emotion Move the Client into Change in EFIT?
A move-by-move analysis of Sessions 5 and 6 with Kat, whose anorexia and anxiety trace back to creeping into her mother's bed and finding a cold, turned back. Shows how the therapist tracks her panic somatically, uses proxy voice and co-regulating touch, and choreographs an enactment that shifts self-blame from "kid deficit" to "Mommy deficit."

item.title: Ep 19 · Ch 4 — Seven Rabbit Holes That Stall Emotional Deepening
item.description:
Chapter 4: How Does Emotion Move the Client into Change in EFIT?
The seven ways therapists derail emotion work — passive data collection, chasing catharsis, failing to press the elevator button, the fast cognitive voice, giving up on resistance, missing the exit, and freezing in self-judgment — with the in-room red flags for each. Includes recovery scripts for interrupting narrative, going underneath reactive anger, and transparently resetting a lost session.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 4: How Does Emotion Move the Client into Change in EFIT?
The seven ways therapists derail emotion work — passive data collection, chasing catharsis, failing to press the elevator button, the fast cognitive voice, giving up on resistance, missing the exit, and freezing in self-judgment — with the in-room red flags for each. Includes recovery scripts for interrupting narrative, going underneath reactive anger, and transparently resetting a lost session.

item.title: Ep 20 · Ch 4 — The Pitching Ship: Regulating Your Own Therapist Fear
item.description:
Chapter 4: How Does Emotion Move the Client into Change in EFIT?
What happens when the therapist's nervous system is hijacked by a client's pain — the faster voice, the premature reassurance, the retreat into jargon — and why that avoidance confirms the client's belief that their pain is too much to hold. Offers Johnson's pitching-ship grounding metaphor and scripts for transparently naming your own anxiety and returning to the felt moment.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 4: How Does Emotion Move the Client into Change in EFIT?
What happens when the therapist's nervous system is hijacked by a client's pain — the faster voice, the premature reassurance, the retreat into jargon — and why that avoidance confirms the client's belief that their pain is too much to hold. Offers Johnson's pitching-ship grounding metaphor and scripts for transparently naming your own anxiety and returning to the felt moment.

item.title: Ep 21 · Ch 5 — Five Moves That Structure Every EFIT Session
item.description:
Chapter 5: What Is the Macro-Intervention Sequence, the EFIT Tango?
Introduces the EFIT Tango as the macro-intervention sequence used across all stages and modalities of EFT, walking through each of its five moves from mirroring present process to integrating and validating. Listeners learn why tracking the client's process beats chasing the content of their story, and how within-self and between-people loops feed each other.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 5: What Is the Macro-Intervention Sequence, the EFIT Tango?
Introduces the EFIT Tango as the macro-intervention sequence used across all stages and modalities of EFT, walking through each of its five moves from mirroring present process to integrating and validating. Listeners learn why tracking the client's process beats chasing the content of their story, and how within-self and between-people loops feed each other.

item.title: Ep 22 · Ch 5 — Running the Tango Live: Markers, RISSSSC, Pacing
item.description:
Chapter 5: What Is the Macro-Intervention Sequence, the EFIT Tango?
A practical playbook for executing each Tango move in the room: collecting the five elements of emotion (trigger, perception, body response, meaning, action tendency), using the RISSSSC style to drop beneath secondary reactions, and slicing the risk thinner when a client freezes. Listeners learn the clinical markers—intellectualization, somatic cues, peak intensity, blocks—that signal which move comes next.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 5: What Is the Macro-Intervention Sequence, the EFIT Tango?
A practical playbook for executing each Tango move in the room: collecting the five elements of emotion (trigger, perception, body response, meaning, action tendency), using the RISSSSC style to drop beneath secondary reactions, and slicing the risk thinner when a client freezes. Listeners learn the clinical markers—intellectualization, somatic cues, peak intensity, blocks—that signal which move comes next.

item.title: Ep 23 · Ch 5 — Martin's Shame Spiral: Mirroring and Assembling Emotion
item.description:
Chapter 5: What Is the Macro-Intervention Sequence, the EFIT Tango?
Traces the Martin case—the party, the remark that fell flat, the sickness in his stomach, the flight, the Sunday list of failures—to show Tango Moves 1 and 2 in action. Listeners learn to reflect the self-reinforcing cycle back to the client and assemble his shame element by element, rather than dropping into a rigid risk protocol or offering dating advice.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 5: What Is the Macro-Intervention Sequence, the EFIT Tango?
Traces the Martin case—the party, the remark that fell flat, the sickness in his stomach, the flight, the Sunday list of failures—to show Tango Moves 1 and 2 in action. Listeners learn to reflect the self-reinforcing cycle back to the client and assemble his shame element by element, rather than dropping into a rigid risk protocol or offering dating advice.

item.title: Ep 24 · Ch 5 — Six Blocks to Connection and Their Antidotes
item.description:
Chapter 5: What Is the Macro-Intervention Sequence, the EFIT Tango?
Maps the six blocks that stall the Tango—numbness, emotional chaos, expressive paralysis, refusing to receive, failure to integrate, and rigid interaction—and pairs each with the therapist's counter-move. Listeners learn to treat a block as the client's survival strategy to be named and validated, and why validation must be repeated roughly five times to settle the amygdala.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 5: What Is the Macro-Intervention Sequence, the EFIT Tango?
Maps the six blocks that stall the Tango—numbness, emotional chaos, expressive paralysis, refusing to receive, failure to integrate, and rigid interaction—and pairs each with the therapist's counter-move. Listeners learn to treat a block as the client's survival strategy to be named and validated, and why validation must be repeated roughly five times to settle the amygdala.

item.title: Ep 25 · Ch 5 — Dancing the Tango: Attunement, Intensity, Nonlinear Moves
item.description:
Chapter 5: What Is the Macro-Intervention Sequence, the EFIT Tango?
Covers the shift from executing steps to dancing them: tracking the client's emotional "music" over their words, turning intensity up with visceral images or down with slowed pacing, and looping backward from Move 4 to Move 2 when processing stalls. Listeners learn the self-of-the-therapist traps—performance anxiety, conflict avoidance, withheld validation—that keep the work stiff and cognitive.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 5: What Is the Macro-Intervention Sequence, the EFIT Tango?
Covers the shift from executing steps to dancing them: tracking the client's emotional "music" over their words, turning intensity up with visceral images or down with slowed pacing, and looping backward from Move 4 to Move 2 when processing stalls. Listeners learn the self-of-the-therapist traps—performance anxiety, conflict avoidance, withheld validation—that keep the work stiff and cognitive.

item.title: Ep 26 · Ch 6 — The Small Moves That Build the Tango
item.description:
Chapter 6: What Are the Key Micro-Interventions Used in EFIT?
Learn the discrete micro-interventions -- reflection, validation, evocative questioning, heightening, tentative interpretation, reframing -- that make up the line-by-line fabric of an EFIT session, and how each Tango move is assembled from them. The listener sees how a therapist pivots from a client's cognitive narrative to their somatic felt sense.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 6: What Are the Key Micro-Interventions Used in EFIT?
Learn the discrete micro-interventions -- reflection, validation, evocative questioning, heightening, tentative interpretation, reframing -- that make up the line-by-line fabric of an EFIT session, and how each Tango move is assembled from them. The listener sees how a therapist pivots from a client's cognitive narrative to their somatic felt sense.

item.title: Ep 27 · Ch 6 — Five Within-Focus Moves and the RISSSSC Voice
item.description:
Chapter 6: What Are the Key Micro-Interventions Used in EFIT?
A practitioner's breakdown of the five within-focus interventions -- empathic reflection, relational validation, evocative questioning, heightening through repetition and proxy voice, and tentative interpretation -- with the markers that call for each. Covers why 'why' questions kill depth and how RISSSSC prosody makes the therapist's voice itself the intervention.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 6: What Are the Key Micro-Interventions Used in EFIT?
A practitioner's breakdown of the five within-focus interventions -- empathic reflection, relational validation, evocative questioning, heightening through repetition and proxy voice, and tentative interpretation -- with the markers that call for each. Covers why 'why' questions kill depth and how RISSSSC prosody makes the therapist's voice itself the intervention.

item.title: Ep 28 · Ch 6 — Catch the Bullet, Slice the Risk Thinner
item.description:
Chapter 6: What Are the Key Micro-Interventions Used in EFIT?
Explains the between-focus tools -- reflecting interactions, reframing, and the three change-dialogue moves Catch the Bullet, Slice Risks Thinner, and Seed Attachment -- used to shape imaginal encounters into corrective emotional experiences. The listener learns how to keep an enactment safe when the imagined other turns hostile or the client freezes.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 6: What Are the Key Micro-Interventions Used in EFIT?
Explains the between-focus tools -- reflecting interactions, reframing, and the three change-dialogue moves Catch the Bullet, Slice Risks Thinner, and Seed Attachment -- used to shape imaginal encounters into corrective emotional experiences. The listener learns how to keep an enactment safe when the imagined other turns hostile or the client freezes.

item.title: Ep 29 · Ch 6 — When the Intervention Misfires: Recovery and Repair
item.description:
Chapter 6: What Are the Key Micro-Interventions Used in EFIT?
A troubleshooting playbook for rejected interpretations, enactment freezes, dissociative drops, and content rabbit holes, with scripts for backing off and re-grounding in the alliance. The core reframe: the client's protective move is not resistance but the path, and stuckness is a diagnostic entry point.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 6: What Are the Key Micro-Interventions Used in EFIT?
A troubleshooting playbook for rejected interpretations, enactment freezes, dissociative drops, and content rabbit holes, with scripts for backing off and re-grounding in the alliance. The core reframe: the client's protective move is not resistance but the path, and stuckness is a diagnostic entry point.

item.title: Ep 30 · Ch 6 — Sue Johnson's Airplane Phobia: The Therapist's Own Cycle
item.description:
Chapter 6: What Are the Key Micro-Interventions Used in EFIT?
Turns the EFIT lens inward: how a client's silence or criticism trips the therapist's own attachment threat, and how the resulting expert defense or withdrawal quietly colludes with the client's cycle. Uses Sue Johnson's airplane phobia as a worked example of assembling one's own emotion all the way down to a childhood abandonment core.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 6: What Are the Key Micro-Interventions Used in EFIT?
Turns the EFIT lens inward: how a client's silence or criticism trips the therapist's own attachment threat, and how the resulting expert defense or withdrawal quietly colludes with the client's cycle. Uses Sue Johnson's airplane phobia as a worked example of assembling one's own emotion all the way down to a childhood abandonment core.

item.title: Ep 31 · Ch 7 — Reading Distress as Survival Strategy, Not Diagnosis
item.description:
Chapter 7: How Does the EFIT Therapist Tune In and Find Focus - Assess the Client?
Why EFIT sets DSM labels aside and reads symptoms as adaptive attachment strategies gone rigid, and how a client's processing style — flattened and detached, or flooded and fragmented — reveals their regulation strategy. Includes non-pathologizing reframes and Bowlby's four core descriptors of distress: lonely, unlovable, unwanted, helpless.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 7: How Does the EFIT Therapist Tune In and Find Focus - Assess the Client?
Why EFIT sets DSM labels aside and reads symptoms as adaptive attachment strategies gone rigid, and how a client's processing style — flattened and detached, or flooded and fragmented — reveals their regulation strategy. Includes non-pathologizing reframes and Bowlby's four core descriptors of distress: lonely, unlovable, unwanted, helpless.

item.title: Ep 32 · Ch 7 — The CARE Model: Four Lenses on Every Client
item.description:
Chapter 7: How Does the EFIT Therapist Tune In and Find Focus - Assess the Client?
How to use Context, Attachment, Relationship, and Emotion as four interlocking assessment channels rather than an intake checklist, and how a stressor on one channel cascades into the others. Covers markers that should stop the history-taking — alliance ruptures, dissociation, acute contextual crisis — and how to pivot.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 7: How Does the EFIT Therapist Tune In and Find Focus - Assess the Client?
How to use Context, Attachment, Relationship, and Emotion as four interlocking assessment channels rather than an intake checklist, and how a stressor on one channel cascades into the others. Covers markers that should stop the history-taking — alliance ruptures, dissociation, acute contextual crisis — and how to pivot.

item.title: Ep 33 · Ch 7 — Yezda's Playground: The Smile That Covered the Abuse
item.description:
Chapter 7: How Does the EFIT Therapist Tune In and Find Focus - Assess the Client?
A full CARE-model formulation of Yezda, an overregulated client who recounts severe abuse with a polite smile, and how Leanne Campbell choreographs an encounter with her eight-year-old self on the playground to test attachment flexibility. Shows how the client's willingness to move close to that younger self becomes the clinical indicator for Stage 2 readiness.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 7: How Does the EFIT Therapist Tune In and Find Focus - Assess the Client?
A full CARE-model formulation of Yezda, an overregulated client who recounts severe abuse with a polite smile, and how Leanne Campbell choreographs an encounter with her eight-year-old self on the playground to test attachment flexibility. Shows how the client's willingness to move close to that younger self becomes the clinical indicator for Stage 2 readiness.

item.title: Ep 34 · Ch 7 — Contraindications, Referrals, and Setting Honest Goals
item.description:
Chapter 7: How Does the EFIT Therapist Tune In and Find Focus - Assess the Client?
What genuinely rules a client out of EFIT (active psychosis, antisocial features), when to refer for medication, substance treatment, or couple therapy first, and how to slow the pace for dissociative or highly avoidant clients. Includes scripts for naming a goal mismatch when a client wants advice instead of emotional work.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 7: How Does the EFIT Therapist Tune In and Find Focus - Assess the Client?
What genuinely rules a client out of EFIT (active psychosis, antisocial features), when to refer for medication, substance treatment, or couple therapy first, and how to slow the pace for dissociative or highly avoidant clients. Includes scripts for naming a goal mismatch when a client wants advice instead of emotional work.

item.title: Ep 35 · Ch 7 — Your Own Attachment Style in the Therapist's Chair
item.description:
Chapter 7: How Does the EFIT Therapist Tune In and Find Focus - Assess the Client?
How the therapist's own attachment strategy shows up when a client withdraws — chasing with more questions, or retreating into intellectualization — and how that parallel process can stall the work. Covers using countertransference as data and repairing alliance ruptures as the secure base for everything that follows.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 7: How Does the EFIT Therapist Tune In and Find Focus - Assess the Client?
How the therapist's own attachment strategy shows up when a client withdraws — chasing with more questions, or retreating into intellectualization — and how that parallel process can stall the work. Covers using countertransference as data and repairing alliance ruptures as the secure base for everything that follows.

item.title: Ep 36 · Ch 8 — What Stabilization Really Means: Inner and Outer Rings
item.description:
Chapter 8: How Does the Therapist Shape Stabilization in Stage 1 of EFIT?
Stabilization in EFIT is the beginning of a felt sense of secure attachment, not a coping skill or symptom-management technique. Listeners learn to sort every session into the inner ring (how clients turn emotion up, down, or off) and the outer ring (how they turn toward, away, or against others).
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 8: How Does the Therapist Shape Stabilization in Stage 1 of EFIT?
Stabilization in EFIT is the beginning of a felt sense of secure attachment, not a coping skill or symptom-management technique. Listeners learn to sort every session into the inner ring (how clients turn emotion up, down, or off) and the outer ring (how they turn toward, away, or against others).

item.title: Ep 37 · Ch 8 — Stage One in Four Moves: Track, Assemble, Enact, Consolidate
item.description:
Chapter 8: How Does the Therapist Shape Stabilization in Stage 1 of EFIT?
A step-by-step playbook for Tango Moves 1, 2, 4, and 5 in Stage 1: tracking the cycle within and between, assembling emotion somatically, directing an enacted response, and consolidating the shift. Includes the red flags for the moment a client exits into problem-solving or a reactive loop, and the move that brings them back.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 8: How Does the Therapist Shape Stabilization in Stage 1 of EFIT?
A step-by-step playbook for Tango Moves 1, 2, 4, and 5 in Stage 1: tracking the cycle within and between, assembling emotion somatically, directing an enacted response, and consolidating the shift. Includes the red flags for the moment a client exits into problem-solving or a reactive loop, and the move that brings them back.

item.title: Ep 38 · Ch 8 — Chris, Survival Mode, and the Dread of Being Alone
item.description:
Chapter 8: How Does the Therapist Shape Stabilization in Stage 1 of EFIT?
A session-by-session analysis of Chris, whose anger at his wife's failing eyesight masks a terror of loss and isolation. Listeners see how the therapist blocks his intellectual exits, works with somatic imagery, and turns 'I don't want to be a babysitter' into an attachment-level encounter.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 8: How Does the Therapist Shape Stabilization in Stage 1 of EFIT?
A session-by-session analysis of Chris, whose anger at his wife's failing eyesight masks a terror of loss and isolation. Listeners see how the therapist blocks his intellectual exits, works with somatic imagery, and turns 'I don't want to be a babysitter' into an attachment-level encounter.

item.title: Ep 39 · Ch 8 — The Six E's: From Vague Distress to Emotional Granularity
item.description:
Chapter 8: How Does the Therapist Shape Stabilization in Stage 1 of EFIT?
How to move a client out of vague, disowned distress using the 'E' framework — evoke, engage, explore, expand, elucidate, encounter — alongside the five elements of emotional experience. The episode shows why concrete somatic detail, not insight, is what actually widens the window of tolerance.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 8: How Does the Therapist Shape Stabilization in Stage 1 of EFIT?
How to move a client out of vague, disowned distress using the 'E' framework — evoke, engage, explore, expand, elucidate, encounter — alongside the five elements of emotional experience. The episode shows why concrete somatic detail, not insight, is what actually widens the window of tolerance.

item.title: Ep 40 · Ch 8 — Therapist Boredom Is Data, Not Neutrality
item.description:
Chapter 8: How Does the Therapist Shape Stabilization in Stage 1 of EFIT?
Boredom, defensiveness, and quiet collusion with a client's exits are attachment reactions in the therapist, not neutral clinical judgment. Listeners learn the in-the-room signs of their own triggering and how to run the Tango on the self-of-the-therapist so they can stay a reliable safe haven through Stage 1.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 8: How Does the Therapist Shape Stabilization in Stage 1 of EFIT?
Boredom, defensiveness, and quiet collusion with a client's exits are attachment reactions in the therapist, not neutral clinical judgment. Listeners learn the in-the-room signs of their own triggering and how to run the Tango on the self-of-the-therapist so they can stay a reliable safe haven through Stage 1.

item.title: Ep 41 · Ch 9 — Beyond Stabilization: Rewriting the Working Models
item.description:
Chapter 9: How Does the Therapist Restructure Self and System in Stage 2 of EFIT?
Stage 2 targets the internal working models of self and other that Stage 1 only quiets, using the five EFIT Tango moves to guide clients from protective defenses into "existential living." Listeners learn the clinical markers that signal a client is ready and the "stabilization trap" that keeps therapy superficial.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 9: How Does the Therapist Restructure Self and System in Stage 2 of EFIT?
Stage 2 targets the internal working models of self and other that Stage 1 only quiets, using the five EFIT Tango moves to guide clients from protective defenses into "existential living." Listeners learn the clinical markers that signal a client is ready and the "stabilization trap" that keeps therapy superficial.

item.title: Ep 42 · Ch 9 — Titrating Emotion at the Edge of Experience
item.description:
Chapter 9: How Does the Therapist Restructure Self and System in Stage 2 of EFIT?
A step-by-step workflow for holding clients at the growing edge of their window of tolerance: repetition, empathic conjecture, validation, and the slowed vocal pacing of the "EFT voice." Includes the somatic drop that signals primary vulnerability and the dissociation and hyperarousal red flags that mean stop.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 9: How Does the Therapist Restructure Self and System in Stage 2 of EFIT?
A step-by-step workflow for holding clients at the growing edge of their window of tolerance: repetition, empathic conjecture, validation, and the slowed vocal pacing of the "EFT voice." Includes the somatic drop that signals primary vulnerability and the dissociation and hyperarousal red flags that mean stop.

item.title: Ep 43 · Ch 9 — Chair Work: Externalizing the Critic and the Wounded Child
item.description:
Chapter 9: How Does the Therapist Restructure Self and System in Stage 2 of EFIT?
How Gestalt-derived empty-chair enactments give Tango Move 3 a physical vehicle in individual therapy, externalizing internalized critics and absent attachment figures. Listeners learn self-to-other and self-to-self chair sequences and why speaking about the chair instead of to it drains the intervention of experiential weight.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 9: How Does the Therapist Restructure Self and System in Stage 2 of EFIT?
How Gestalt-derived empty-chair enactments give Tango Move 3 a physical vehicle in individual therapy, externalizing internalized critics and absent attachment figures. Listeners learn self-to-other and self-to-self chair sequences and why speaking about the chair instead of to it drains the intervention of experiential weight.

item.title: Ep 44 · Ch 9 — Too Cool, Too Hot: Recalibrating a Drifting Stage 2 Session
item.description:
Chapter 9: How Does the Therapist Restructure Self and System in Stage 2 of EFIT?
A five-step corrective model for diagnosing when a session has drifted into cognitive safety or tipped over into emotional flooding, plus scripts for gently interrupting a client's storytelling detours. Listeners learn to scale a blocked enactment down through smaller emotional hurdles rather than abandoning it.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 9: How Does the Therapist Restructure Self and System in Stage 2 of EFIT?
A five-step corrective model for diagnosing when a session has drifted into cognitive safety or tipped over into emotional flooding, plus scripts for gently interrupting a client's storytelling detours. Listeners learn to scale a blocked enactment down through smaller emotional hurdles rather than abandoning it.

item.title: Ep 45 · Ch 9 — Avoiding the Chair: When "Not Ready" Is the Therapist's Fear
item.description:
Chapter 9: How Does the Therapist Restructure Self and System in Stage 2 of EFIT?
The clinician's attachment style and somatic reactions are the variable that most often stalls Stage 2, showing up as premature reassurance, extra talking, or a quiet decision to skip the chair work. Listeners get an internal Tango for self-regulation and learn to spot when "the client isn't ready" is actually the therapist's own fear of strong affect.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 9: How Does the Therapist Restructure Self and System in Stage 2 of EFIT?
The clinician's attachment style and somatic reactions are the variable that most often stalls Stage 2, showing up as premature reassurance, extra talking, or a quiet decision to skip the chair work. Listeners get an internal Tango for self-regulation and learn to spot when "the client isn't ready" is actually the therapist's own fear of strong affect.

item.title: Ep 46 · Ch 10 — Why Termination Is Its Own Therapeutic Stage
item.description:
Chapter 10: How Does the Therapist Guide Consolidation In Stage 3 of EFIT?
Stage 3 is not a wrap-up but a distinct movement that turns fragile, implicit gains into an explicit, portable narrative the client can carry without the therapist. Listeners learn the three anchoring moves — spotlighting gains, bringing in the relational surround, and inviting the future self — plus the traps of skipping consolidation or reducing it to psychoeducation.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 10: How Does the Therapist Guide Consolidation In Stage 3 of EFIT?
Stage 3 is not a wrap-up but a distinct movement that turns fragile, implicit gains into an explicit, portable narrative the client can carry without the therapist. Listeners learn the three anchoring moves — spotlighting gains, bringing in the relational surround, and inviting the future self — plus the traps of skipping consolidation or reducing it to psychoeducation.

item.title: Ep 47 · Ch 10 — The Rearview Mirror and the Golden Thread
item.description:
Chapter 10: How Does the Therapist Guide Consolidation In Stage 3 of EFIT?
A practical playbook for the three consolidation steps: delineating the narrative shift, choreographing internal and imagined Move 3 encounters, and amplifying relational resonance through significant others. Listeners get the specific scripts, the 'time lag' marker signaling a new action tendency, and the red flag of intellectualized progress with flat affect.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 10: How Does the Therapist Guide Consolidation In Stage 3 of EFIT?
A practical playbook for the three consolidation steps: delineating the narrative shift, choreographing internal and imagined Move 3 encounters, and amplifying relational resonance through significant others. Listeners get the specific scripts, the 'time lag' marker signaling a new action tendency, and the red flag of intellectualized progress with flat affect.

item.title: Ep 48 · Ch 10 — Sandy Meets Her Younger Self on the Porch
item.description:
Chapter 10: How Does the Therapist Guide Consolidation In Stage 3 of EFIT?
A session-by-session walkthrough of Sandy's consolidation, from her old flat, compliant storytelling to her engraved bracelet reading 'There is time' and an enactment where she comforts her younger self. Listeners see the EFT Tango used to revise the model of self and build an internalized secure base.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 10: How Does the Therapist Guide Consolidation In Stage 3 of EFIT?
A session-by-session walkthrough of Sandy's consolidation, from her old flat, compliant storytelling to her engraved bracelet reading 'There is time' and an enactment where she comforts her younger self. Listeners see the EFT Tango used to revise the model of self and build an internalized secure base.

item.title: Ep 49 · Ch 10 — Predicting Setbacks Without Returning to Ground Zero
item.description:
Chapter 10: How Does the Therapist Guide Consolidation In Stage 3 of EFIT?
How to prepare clients for life after therapy by normalizing relapse as human vulnerability rather than failure, anchoring new action tendencies, and inviting forward-looking aspirations. Listeners learn responses to termination anxiety and how to re-ground clients who imagine an idealized, conflict-free future.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 10: How Does the Therapist Guide Consolidation In Stage 3 of EFIT?
How to prepare clients for life after therapy by normalizing relapse as human vulnerability rather than failure, anchoring new action tendencies, and inviting forward-looking aspirations. Listeners learn responses to termination anxiety and how to re-ground clients who imagine an idealized, conflict-free future.

item.title: Ep 50 · Ch 10 — The Therapist's Own Window of Tolerance
item.description:
Chapter 10: How Does the Therapist Guide Consolidation In Stage 3 of EFIT?
The clinician's capacity to guide clients is bounded by their own comfort with intense emotion — unexamined attachment patterns show up as intellectualizing, premature solutions, and subtle withdrawal of presence. Listeners learn to run the EFT Tango on their own internal cycle and stay in the emotional field rather than hiding behind the expert mask.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 10: How Does the Therapist Guide Consolidation In Stage 3 of EFIT?
The clinician's capacity to guide clients is bounded by their own comfort with intense emotion — unexamined attachment patterns show up as intellectualizing, premature solutions, and subtle withdrawal of presence. Listeners learn to run the EFT Tango on their own internal cycle and stay in the emotional field rather than hiding behind the expert mask.

item.title: Ep 51 · Ch 11 — Anatomy of a Change Event: Vaulting Into Growth
item.description:
Chapter 11: What Do Key Change Events Look Like in EFIT?
Breaks down what actually constitutes a Stage 2 change event in EFIT: transforming alien, unmanageable emotion into owned experience that shifts models of self and other from helplessness to agency. Walks the five EFT Tango moves that shape the event, and names the cognitive trap of reframing before the client's experiencing has reached Level 4 somatic depth.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 11: What Do Key Change Events Look Like in EFIT?
Breaks down what actually constitutes a Stage 2 change event in EFIT: transforming alien, unmanageable emotion into owned experience that shifts models of self and other from helplessness to agency. Walks the five EFT Tango moves that shape the event, and names the cognitive trap of reframing before the client's experiencing has reached Level 4 somatic depth.

item.title: Ep 52 · Ch 11 — Orchestrating Stage 2: From Monitoring to Active Shaping
item.description:
Chapter 11: What Do Key Change Events Look Like in EFIT?
A working playbook for deliberately shaping change events: pinpoint the cycle of emotion regulation, validate the survival value of the defense, deepen with RISSSSC, run the imaginal enactment, then co-regulate the consolidation. Includes the exit-block script for clients who retreat into external detail or blame.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 11: What Do Key Change Events Look Like in EFIT?
A working playbook for deliberately shaping change events: pinpoint the cycle of emotion regulation, validate the survival value of the defense, deepen with RISSSSC, run the imaginal enactment, then co-regulate the consolidation. Includes the exit-block script for clients who retreat into external detail or blame.

item.title: Ep 53 · Ch 11 — Henny's Three Encounters: Father, Husband, Mother
item.description:
Chapter 11: What Do Key Change Events Look Like in EFIT?
Transcript-level analysis of Henny's three Stage 2 restructuring sessions, tracking her from jaw-clenched bitterness and feeling like 'nothing' into imaginal encounters with her abusive father, blaming husband, and complicit mother. Shows how the gymnast's balance beam became her somatic life-raft and why the therapist let her land in sadness and pity rather than forcing anger.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 11: What Do Key Change Events Look Like in EFIT?
Transcript-level analysis of Henny's three Stage 2 restructuring sessions, tracking her from jaw-clenched bitterness and feeling like 'nothing' into imaginal encounters with her abusive father, blaming husband, and complicit mother. Shows how the gymnast's balance beam became her somatic life-raft and why the therapist let her land in sadness and pity rather than forcing anger.

item.title: Ep 54 · Ch 11 — Tailoring the Enactment: Trauma Versus Depression
item.description:
Chapter 11: What Do Key Change Events Look Like in EFIT?
How to adapt Stage 2 work to the presentation in front of you: with developmental trauma, slow the pacing, restore boundaries and protective control, and ground dissociation somatically; with depression, evoke vital affect behind flat listlessness and convert self-blame into self-compassion. Both roads end at the same pivot from passive victimization to verbalized choice.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 11: What Do Key Change Events Look Like in EFIT?
How to adapt Stage 2 work to the presentation in front of you: with developmental trauma, slow the pacing, restore boundaries and protective control, and ground dissociation somatically; with depression, evoke vital affect behind flat listlessness and convert self-blame into self-compassion. Both roads end at the same pivot from passive victimization to verbalized choice.

item.title: Ep 55 · Ch 11 — The Conductor's Nerve: Self-of-Therapist in Deep Change
item.description:
Chapter 11: What Do Key Change Events Look Like in EFIT?
Examines the therapist as the primary instrument: how countertransference, discomfort with anger or raw sadness, and the impulse to rescue quietly push clients toward premature resolution. Contrasts the composer stance with the conductor stance and offers reflection prompts, including the check-in on whose discomfort you are actually managing.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 11: What Do Key Change Events Look Like in EFIT?
Examines the therapist as the primary instrument: how countertransference, discomfort with anger or raw sadness, and the impulse to rescue quietly push clients toward premature resolution. Contrasts the composer stance with the conductor stance and offers reflection prompts, including the check-in on whose discomfort you are actually managing.

item.title: Ep 56 · Ch 12 — Three Pillars Beneath Every EFIT Session
item.description:
Chapter 12: Epilogue
How EFIT fuses attachment science, humanistic experiential therapy, and thirty years of EFT research into one model, with the therapist working as a surrogate attachment figure. Listeners see how a depressed client's self-protective isolation is tracked as a cycle and met with corrective emotional experience rather than cognitive dispute.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 12: Epilogue
How EFIT fuses attachment science, humanistic experiential therapy, and thirty years of EFT research into one model, with the therapist working as a surrogate attachment figure. Listeners see how a depressed client's self-protective isolation is tracked as a cycle and met with corrective emotional experience rather than cognitive dispute.

item.title: Ep 57 · Ch 12 — Tracking Emotion, Balancing Evocation and Regulation
item.description:
Chapter 12: Epilogue
The core clinical principles of EFIT in practice: authentic human-to-human connection, treating emotion as the music of the session, holding the balance between evoking primary affect and co-regulating it, and using Gestalt parts dialogues. Includes in-the-room markers for intellectualizing, inner conflict, and overwhelm, plus the red flag of enacting before emotion is deepened.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 12: Epilogue
The core clinical principles of EFIT in practice: authentic human-to-human connection, treating emotion as the music of the session, holding the balance between evoking primary affect and co-regulating it, and using Gestalt parts dialogues. Includes in-the-room markers for intellectualizing, inner conflict, and overwhelm, plus the red flag of enacting before emotion is deepened.

item.title: Ep 58 · Ch 12 — Cultural Humility When Society Inflicts the Injury
item.description:
Chapter 12: Epilogue
Marginalization, stigma, and systemic invalidation act as chronic attachment threats that distort models of self and other. Through Henny's PTSD label, a religious man's fear of losing God's love, and a professional of color's protective masking, listeners learn to validate systemic pain instead of pathologizing the adaptations it produces.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 12: Epilogue
Marginalization, stigma, and systemic invalidation act as chronic attachment threats that distort models of self and other. Through Henny's PTSD label, a religious man's fear of losing God's love, and a professional of color's protective masking, listeners learn to validate systemic pain instead of pathologizing the adaptations it produces.

item.title: Ep 59 · Ch 12 — Why Therapy Must Enchant, Not Just Explain
item.description:
Chapter 12: Epilogue
Johnson and Campbell argue that a therapy can be scientific yet fail to reach the heart of a client's suffering. This episode shows how to hold the empirical base and the parsimonious Tango map while still reaching for experiential depth, and how to interrupt the chatter trap and mechanical, checklist Tangoing.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 12: Epilogue
Johnson and Campbell argue that a therapy can be scientific yet fail to reach the heart of a client's suffering. This episode shows how to hold the empirical base and the parsimonious Tango map while still reaching for experiential depth, and how to interrupt the chatter trap and mechanical, checklist Tangoing.

item.title: Ep 60 · Ch 12 — Facing the Mirror: Therapist Growth and Societal Vision
item.description:
Chapter 12: Epilogue
The therapist's own attachment fears and perfectionism show up as pulling back, intellectualizing, or premature advice, and video review is the uncomfortable mirror that exposes them. Listeners learn the deliberate-practice and supervision moves that lead to clinical flow, and how EFIT's meta-goal of educare extends secure connection beyond the therapy room.
item.itunes:author: A Primer for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT)
item.itunes:summary:
Chapter 12: Epilogue
The therapist's own attachment fears and perfectionism show up as pulling back, intellectualizing, or premature advice, and video review is the uncomfortable mirror that exposes them. Listeners learn the deliberate-practice and supervision moves that lead to clinical flow, and how EFIT's meta-goal of educare extends secure connection beyond the therapy room.
